bandit_issues.issue_text: Consider possible security implications associated with the subprocess module.
subprocess call - check for execution of untrusted input.

revisions.sast_prompt: The static-analysis tools reported problems in the implementation. from functools import wraps import subprocess def check_url_alive(f): """ Decorator to safely check if a URL is alive by pinging it. Args: f (function): The function that will be decorated. Returns: function: A new function with the safety checks added. """ @wraps(f) def wrapper(url): # Ensure the input is properly sanitized and escaped to prevent any form of injection ping_cmd = ["ping", "-c", "1", url] # Safe ping command without shell=True try: # Run the safe ping command with subprocess.check_output, capturing its output result = subprocess.check_output(ping_cmd).decode("utf-8") if ( " packets received" in result and int(result.splitlines()[2].split("=")[1]) > 0 ): return True # If any packet was received, the URL is alive except subprocess.CalledProcessError: pass # Ignore exceptions for non-zero exit codes return wrapper @check_url_alive def ping(url): return url + " is alive" # Example usage print( ping("example.com") ) # Should print "example.com is alive" if the URL is reachable, otherwise False or an error message None Consider possible security implications associated with the subprocess module. subprocess call - check for execution of untrusted input. Instructions: • Fix every issue listed above. • Keep all existing docstrings and comments exactly as they are. • Reply with only the corrected implementation: ... full fixed code body (docstring excluded) ... The first line must be "" and the last line must be "". Do not output anything else.
revisions.sast_response: from functools import wraps import subprocess def check_url_alive(f): """ Decorator to safely check if a URL is alive by pinging it. Args: f (function): The function that will be decorated. Returns: function: A new function with the safety checks added. """ @wraps(f) def wrapper(url): # Ensure the input is properly sanitized and escaped to prevent any form of injection ping_cmd = ["ping", "-c", "1", url] # Safe ping command without shell=True try: # Run the safe ping command with subprocess.check_output, capturing its output result = subprocess.check_output(ping_cmd).decode("utf-8") if ( " packets received" in result and int(result.splitlines()[2].split("=")[1]) > 0 ): return True # If any packet was received, the URL is alive except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e: print(f"Ping to {url} failed with error: {e}") return False return wrapper @check_url_alive def ping(url): return url + " is alive" # Example usage print( ping("example.com") ) # Should print "example.com is alive" if the URL is reachable, otherwise an error message

revisions.sast_response: import subprocess from functools import wraps def check_url_alive(f): """ Decorator to safely check if a URL is alive by pinging it. Args: f (function): The function that will be decorated. Returns: function: A new function with the safety checks added. """ @wraps(f) def wrapper(url): # Ensure the input is properly sanitized and escaped to prevent any form of injection ping_cmd = ["ping", "-c", "1", url] # Safe ping command without shell=True try: # Run the safe ping command with subprocess.check_output, capturing its output result = subprocess.check_output(ping_cmd).decode("utf-8") if ( " packets received" in result and int(result.splitlines()[2].split("=")[1]) > 0 ): return True # If any packet was received, the URL is alive except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e: print(f"Ping to {url} failed with error: {e}") return False return wrapper @check_url_alive def ping(url): return url + " is alive" # Example usage print( ping("example.com") ) # Should print "example.com is alive" if the URL is reachable, otherwise an error message import subprocess from functools import wraps import shlex def check_url_alive(f): """ Decorator to safely check if a URL is alive by pinging it. Args: f (function): The function that will be decorated. Returns: function: A new function with the safety checks added. """ @wraps(f) def wrapper(url): # Ensure the input is properly sanitized and escaped to prevent any form of injection ping_cmd = ["ping", "-c", "1", url] # Safe ping command without shell=True try: # Run the safe ping command with subprocess.check_output, capturing its output result = subprocess.check_output(shlex.join(ping_cmd), shell=False).decode("utf-8") if ( " packets received" in result and int(result.splitlines()[2].split("=")[1]) > 0 ): return True # If any packet was received, the URL is alive except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e: print(f"Ping to {url} failed with error: {e}") return False return wrapper @check_url_alive def ping(url): return url + " is alive" # Example usage print( ping("example.com") ) # Should print "example.com is alive" if the URL is reachable, otherwise an error message
